Playing with fireworks can be fun but also dangerous if not handled properly. Here are key safety tips to ensure safe firework use:

1. Choose a Safe Location

Open Area: Set off fireworks in a large, open space away from homes, dry grass, trees, or any flammable materials.

Fireproof Surface: Use a flat, hard surface like pavement.

Keep Distance: Ensure bystanders are far away from the launch area.

2. Handle Fireworks Safely

Follow Instructions: Always read and follow the instructions on the fireworks packaging.

Light One at a Time: Light fireworks one at a time and then quickly move away.

Never Relight Duds: If a firework doesnât go off, donât try to relight it. Wait for a few minutes, then soak it in water.

3. Have Safety Equipment Ready

Water: Keep a bucket of water or a hose nearby to extinguish any accidental fires.

Protective Gear: Wear safety glasses to protect your eyes from sparks or debris.

First Aid Kit: Have a first aid kit ready in case of minor injuries.

4. Supervise Children

No Handling Fireworks: Never let young children handle fireworks. Sparklers, often considered safe, can burn at high temperatures.

Supervision: Older children should only use fireworks under close adult supervision.

5. Dispose of Fireworks Properly

Douse with Water: After fireworks have finished burning, douse them with water to prevent fires.

Dispose Safely: Place used fireworks in a metal trash can, away from buildings or flammable items.

Following these tips can help prevent accidents and ensure a fun, safe fireworks display.

Understanding Your Babyâs Nighttime Awakeningsâ¬ï¸

Is your baby waking at 10pm, 12am, 3:30am, 4:30am and 5am?

ð Save this for later

Letâs talk about your babyâs nighttime awakenings. Itâs crucial to understand that everyone, including babies, wakes up during the night. Even if it seems like your baby is sleeping through the night, theyâre actually waking up and smoothly drifting back to sleep without needing your assistance.

As a sleep consultant, I often recognise patterns rooted in sleep science. Understanding these patterns allows me to identify potential reasons behind night wakings. With this insight, we can make adjustments to enhance sleep patterns and discover sustainable solutions for your family.

I commonly see this pattern when babies transition from newborn sleep to a more mature, adult-like sleep. 

When they make this transition, they begin to experience additional lighter stages of sleep, which they havenât had before. This often affects their sleep patterns.

Hereâs what you might observe: 
â±ï¸ False Start: Your baby may wake within 30-45 minutes of bedtime. 
ð 2-Hour Wake: Another waking episode around 2 hours later. 
ð Repeat Wake: Another wake-up 2 hours later... again. 
ð Frequent Wakes: Waking up frequently from 3 am onward, every 45 minutes until morning.

Babies experience these wakings due to a mix of transitioning sleep cycles, sleep associations, and developmental stages.

Now, letâs discuss the 4-Month Sleep regression ! Did you know it can happen anywhere between 3-5 months? So not always at 4 months!!!

Ummm honeyâ¦.see I have this headache andâ¦..ð¤£ð¤£ on a serious note..health anxiety can be so debilitating. I struggled for years and there were times it really took over my life. It lead to serious panic attacks that landed me in the ER. 

I had to really work on myself and find the right supplement to balance my serotonin, dopamine and GABA. 

These are the three things I did that have helped me tremendously. 

1. EFT Tapping..you can find it on YouTube

2. 4-7-8 breathing. Breathe in for 4, hold for 7 and exhale for 8 breaths. 

3. Happy Juice. It is clinically proven to regulate your happy hormones (serotonin, dopamine and GABA). It also regulates cortisol. 

With these 3 things I am anxiety free and panic attack free! 

Comment HAPPY for the supplement I use!
